entry.title: Re: Configuring Reason Codes and Action Codes for France B2B Supplier Invoices in SAP
entry.content:
Hi @PM748 and @GCET 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, SAP provides an API that customers can use to update the status of an incoming eDocument from outside eDocument cockpit. The supported eDocument response statuses/actions can be triggered programmatically using the class CL_EDOC_API_INCOM_RESPONSE.

This API is intended to facilitate integration scenarios where the invoice processing actions (such as Accept, Reject, Dispute, Suspend, or Partially Accept) are performed outside the eDocument Cockpit. Customers can invoke the appropriate methods provided by CL_EDOC_API_INCOM_RESPONSE to set the corresponding supported eDocument status based on the outcome of the external invoice processing.

entry.title: Agent-led Toolchain with SAP
entry.content:
Summary

This article provides structured decision guidance for designing and adopting an agent-led SAP toolchain to support SAP S/4HANA and broader Intelligent Enterprise transformation initiatives.

With SAP LeanIX, SAP Signavio, SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Integration Suite, SAP Build, and SAP Enterprise Continuous Testing by Tricentis, all delivering integrated capabilities across the transformation lifecycle, enterprises must evaluate how these tools work together and make deliberate decisions about adoption scope, governance, and operating model.

The agent-led toolchain goes beyond static tool integration. Drawing on agentic principles, the toolchain reasons over document content, validates inputs, handles exceptions, and triggers follow-on actions across the lifecycle — binding people, processes, and platforms together with transparency and continuous intelligence. This reflects SAP’s vision of a composable, intelligent enterprise where tools do not merely exchange data but actively coordinate transformation outcomes.

Licensing aspects of the solution components discussed are explicitly out of scope for this evaluation.

 

Business Context

1.1 Transformation Context

Organizations undertaking SAP S/4HANA transformations face a common set of challenges:

  • Complex application landscapes with overlapping functionality
  • Fragmented process documentation and ownership
  • Limited visibility of end-to-end business processes and their technical realization
  • Multiple ALM and EA tools with poor integration
  • Pressure to adhere to clean core principles and SAP standard best practices
  • Need to enable transition to Autonomous Enterprise and support continuous innovation in AI, automation, and analytics while controlling risk.

SAP’s agent-led toolchain addresses these challenges by providing a curated set of interoperable tools covering the entire lifecycle, from strategy and architecture to implementation and operations.

The intent is to move away from a fragmented, project-specific tool setup toward a sustainable, enterprise-wide agent-led toolchain that serves every phase of the transformation program across the following lifecycle domains:

  • Business Architecture and Capability Planning: Define and govern the strategic direction
  • Process Design, Standardization, and Improvement: Model, mine, and optimize business processes
  • Program and Project Delivery: Manage requirements, tasks, and deployments
  • Integration and Extensibility Governance: Govern APIs, interfaces, and clean core compliance
  • Test and Quality Management: Automate and risk-align testing
  • Run and Optimize: Monitor operations and drive continuous improvement

1.2 Objectives of the Agent-Led Toolchain

The SAP agent-led toolchain aims to:

  • Break down silos: Seamless data flow and transparency across tools ensure that business, IT, and project teams are aligned and working from the same up-to-date information
  • Drive continuous improvement: Insights from integrated tools reveal bottlenecks, integration gaps, and optimization opportunities, fueling ongoing business and IT agility
  • Accelerate value realization: Faster, better-informed decisions enable organizations to deliver innovation more rapidly and meet evolving business needs
  • Increase decision speed and accuracy: Real-time visibility across business processes, integration architecture, and project progress enables stakeholders to respond quickly and confidently to change
  • Reduce complexity and risk: Streamlining integrations minimizes operational risk and frees IT resources to focus on strategic, high-impact initiatives
  • Enable continuous improvement: Promoting adoption of industry best practices and supporting ongoing digital transformation
  • Future-proof business: Establishing flexible, reusable integration frameworks that support expansion, automation, and technology upgrades
  • Provide end-to-end traceability from business capability to process to application, integration, test, and operations
  • Enable a fit-to-standard, clean core approach to S/4HANA and cloud solutions
  • Industrialize quality assurance through risk-based, integrated test automation
  • Provide a foundation for AI, automation, and intelligent enterprise use cases

1.3 Scope of This Decision

This decision accelerator focuses on the overall agent-led toolchain and its integration. It does not replace detailed product selection or project methodology, but guides on:

  • Understanding how key SAP tools fit together and where they overlap with existing tools
  • Defining criteria for choosing the most appropriate option

 

Solution Building Blocks and Characterization

The SAP-aligned agent-led toolchain consists of the following primary building blocks:

SAP LeanIX

  • Enterprise Architecture Management
  • Business capability modeling, application portfolio management, technology standards, and transformation roadmaps
  • Fact sheets representing applications, processes, interfaces, and IT components
  • Most customer business capabilities can be created as extracts from the SAP Reference Catalog or linked one-to-one for enhanced transformation planning
  • Roadmapping and architecture governance
  • Integration with WalkMe Discovery to automatically feed discovered SaaS applications into the LeanIX SaaS Discovery Inbox

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite

  • Signavio Process Insights: Process performance analysis using SAP ECC and S/4HANA event data
  • Signavio Process Intelligence: Advanced process mining and conformance checking
  • Signavio Process Manager: Process modeling and documentation
  • Signavio Journey Modeler: Customer journey mapping
  • Signavio Governance: Workflow for process approvals and publishing

SAP Cloud ALM

  • Requirements and task management
  • Test case management and execution
  • Deployment orchestration and change tracking
  • Operational monitoring for cloud and hybrid solutions
  • SAP Best Practice template content for fit-to-standard workshop support

SAP Integration Suite

  • API management, Cloud Integration, Event Mesh, and related BTP integration services
  • Integration content and adapters for SAP and non-SAP systems
  • Central monitoring and governance of integration flows
  • Support for event-driven and loosely coupled integration design patterns

SAP Build and SAP Business Technology Platform (sub-components of SAP Business AI Platform)

  • Low-code and no-code application development and workflow automation
  • Process automation and RPA where applicable
  • Side-by-side extensibility on BTP to protect the S/4HANA core
  • Foundation for AI extensions and intelligent enterprise use cases
  • Reusable business services, open-source content including business accelerators, workflow, and integration
  • Cloud-native APIs and microservices enabling reduced time to market

Tricentis (SAP’s Strategic Quality Assurance partner)

  • Test Automation for SAP with SAP Enterprise Continuous Testing by Tricentis
  • Integration with SAP Cloud ALM for test planning and execution reporting
  • Risk-based test coverage capability based on process criticality and business impact with SAP Change Impact Analysis by Tricentis

WalkMe Discovery

  • User behavior analysis and SaaS application discovery; discovered SaaS applications are listed in the LeanIX SaaS Discovery Inbox where they can be reviewed, and new fact sheets can be created, or existing fact sheets can be linked

 SAP Enable Now

  • Training content delivery and user adoption measurement

 

Main Decision Options

1. Option A: (Near-)Full SAP-Native Agent-Led Toolchain

 Description

  1.  Use SAP LeanIX for enterprise architecture management
  2.  Use SAP Signavio suite for process discovery, modeling, mining, and governance
  3.  Use SAP Cloud ALM as the primary ALM for requirements, testing, deployment, and operations
  4.  Use SAP Integration Suite as the standard integration platform
  5.  Use SAP Build and broader BTP services for extensibility and automation
  6.  Use SAP Enterprise Continuous Testing by Tricentis test automation integrated with Cloud ALM and Signavio

Key Integration Aspects

  •  Automatic creation of process-related fact sheets in LeanIX from SAP Signavio models
  •  Bidirectional links between LeanIX applications and Signavio processes, enabling cross-system navigation and reporting
  •  Traceability from requirements in Cloud ALM to processes in Signavio and applications in LeanIX
  •  Test cases in Cloud ALM linked to business processes and executed via Enterprise Continuous Testing by Tricentis
  •  Integration Suite monitored in Cloud ALM with visibility into end-to-end integration flows
  •  WalkMe Discovery automatically populating the LeanIX SaaS Discovery Inbox with discovered SaaS applications

Pros

  • Highest level of end-to-end traceability across the transformation lifecycle
  • Native support for SAP best practices and fit-to-standard transformation approaches
  • Aligned with SAP's strategic roadmap enabling Autonomous Enterprise and reference architectures
  • Better long-term total cost of ownership due to fewer custom integration bridges between tools
  • Enables intelligent enterprise capabilities including AI adoption and automation natively through BTP

Cons

  • Higher upfront adoption effort requiring new tools, training, and operating model changes
  • Requires establishing EA, process management, and ALM disciplines around SAP-native tools
  • May overlap with existing non-SAP tools that are already in use across the organization

 

2. Option B: The “magic trifecta” as a starting point

 Description

  1. Use SAP LeanIX for enterprise architecture management
  2. Use SAP Signavio suite for process discovery, modeling, mining, and governance
  3. Use SAP Cloud ALM as the primary ALM for requirements, testing, deployment, and operations

Key Integration Aspects

  • Automatic creation of process-related fact sheets in LeanIX from SAP Signavio models
  • Bidirectional links between LeanIX applications and Signavio processes, enabling cross-system navigation and reporting
  • Traceability from requirements in Cloud ALM to processes in Signavio and applications in LeanIX

Pros

  • High efficiency due to maximum usage of the available SAP Business Content and Accelerators ( SAP Business Accelerator Hub), automated entity discovery (e.g., SAP Discovery feature in LeanIX when connected to Cloud ALM)
  • Solid technical foundation for effective enterprise data mapping and workflows, even if there is no need or capacity to use every tool immediately
  • Aligned with SAP's strategic roadmap enabling Autonomous Enterprise and reference architectures
  • Contained upfront cost, gradual adoption as the organization becomes ready
  • Less disruptive organizational change management as opposed to full-scale toolchain

Cons

  • Postpones governing of technology layer, user behavioral analysis, data migration tools to a later time
  • Requires establishing EA, process management, and ALM disciplines around SAP-native tools
  • May overlap with existing non-SAP tools that are already in use across the organization

 

3. Option C: Incremental (activate-as-you-go) Toolchain

Description

The approach presumes a high level of retention of existing tools (if permitted by maintenance lifecycle) and ITSM processes, while picking and choosing individual SAP Toolchain tool(s) for activation based on:

  • Addressing gaps or immediate needs in enterprise IT solution governance
  • Preparing for a (better-)controlled transformation of corporate IT solution or an organization-wide program
  • Capitalizing on valid solution entitlements and investments in SAP software

Pros

  • Lowest short-term effort and cost
  • Minimal disruption for organizations with constrained program scope or immature tooling practices
  • Topic-focused relief to identified gaps or immediate ITSM needs

Cons

  • Weak end-to-end traceability across the transformation lifecycle
  • Not suitable for large or strategic SAP programs
  • Higher long-term technical debt and operational risk

 

Decision Tree

Please follow the decision tree below to indicate key considerations and outcomes

 

As seen above, the key criteria for choosing the path are:

  • Is the SaaS solution set acceptable from regulatory and compliance standpoints in the company?
  • Are you solving a tactical-level problem in your ITSM operations or thinking strategically for years ahead (along with a strategic transformation program)?
  • Do you have resources and a transformational window to include the full chain’s activation?
  • Are there any equivalent tools that you want to preserve for any organizational reasons?

Conclusion

A complete SAP AI-led Toolchain in its entirety is your best bet to lay a solid future-proof foundation for your major and minor transformations towards Autonomous Enterprise, streamline the transitions, and elevate the transparency about how every corner of your corporate IT solution is generating value for the company.

The “magic trifecta” SAP LeanIX, Signavio and Cloud ALM would be your quick win in case you need a solid foundation anchored around architecture, business process management, implementations and SAP operations. These tools have accessible reference content and automations to populate repositories with best practices and capture your baseline situation quickly.

If you are after a specific task like automated data migration, automated testing, or agile development, a pragmatic start would be to consider a purpose-specific tool from the Toolchain for activation, but still, one should be aware of how synergies could play out for better efficiency and traceability in the future.

With any choice above, please do consider reaching out to your SAP counterpart (Account Executive, Success Plan Manager, Technical Quality Manager) for assistance with planning, licensing, and execution of your Toolchain journey.

entry.title: Re: Manage Fixed Assets – Main Number of Original Asset -...
entry.content:
Dear @Nozomu_Kihara,

The Legacy Main Number - Subnumber field is not intended as a manually maintained business reference field in the Manage Fixed Assets app. Based on standard behavior, it can be populated during fixed asset migration / legacy asset transfer, but there is no standard option in the Manage Fixed Assets app to enter or maintain this field directly for a newly created regular fixed asset. The reason is that this field is a one-time technical reference populated in the legacy asset transfer / fixed asset migration context, used as part of the legacy data transfer process to retain the reference to the asset number from the source system. It is not part of the standard maintainable screen layout in the Manage Fixed Assets app, and SAP does not provide a supported way to expose this specific field for manual maintenance

More details below:

SAP Help – Fixed asset - Master data
SAP Help – Transfer Legacy Assets with the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit

For a standard field alternative the best fit is usually Inventory Number. It is a standard fixed asset master data field intended for asset identification/tracking purposes and it can be maintained as part of the asset master data. Therefore it is the recommended standard field to store the fixed asset management number from the external system, provided it is not already used for another internal inventory/tagging purpose

More details below:

SAP Help – Manage Fixed Assets
SAP Learning – Maintaining Asset Master Data

The recommended approach is:

  • Do not use Legacy Main Number - Subnumber for ongoing manual maintenance in Manage Fixed Assets
  • Use Inventory Number to store the fixed asset management number from the external system, if this field is available for the customer’s business use
  • If Inventory Number is also reserved or does not meet the required format/length/reporting need then the clean standard extensibility option is to create a dedicated custom field for the external fixed asset reference and expose it in the Manage Fixed Assets app. SAP provides extensibility for fixed asset master data in the Manage Fixed Assets app

More details below:

SAP Help – App Extensibility: Manage Fixed Assets

Best Regards,

Dawid
